2603.17017.title: LLM NL2SQL Robustness: Surface Noise vs. Linguistic Variation in Traditional and Agentic Settings
2603.17017.paper_summary: Robustness evaluation for Natural Language to SQL (NL2SQL) systems is essential because real-world database environments are dynamic, noisy, and continuously evolving, whereas conventional benchmark evaluations typically assume static schemas and well-formed user inputs. In this work, we introduce a robustness evaluation benchmark containing approximately ten types of perturbations and conduct evaluations under both traditional and agentic settings. We assess multiple state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs), including Grok-4.1, Gemini-3-Pro, Claude-Opus-4.6, and GPT-5.2. Our results show that these models generally maintain strong performance under several perturbations; however, notable performance degradation is observed for surface-level noise (e.g., character-level corruption) and linguistic variation that preserves semantics while altering lexical or syntactic forms. Furthermore, we observe that surface-level noise causes larger performance drops in traditional pipelines, whereas linguistic variation presents greater challenges in agentic settings. These findings highlight the remaining challenges in achieving robust NL2SQL systems, particularly in handling linguistic variability.
2603.17017.paper_summary_zh: 穩健性評估對於自然語言到 SQL (NL2SQL) 系統至關重要，因為現實世界的數據庫環境是動態的、嘈雜的並且不斷演變，而傳統的基準評估通常假設靜態的架構和格式良好的用戶輸入。在這項工作中，我們引入了一個包含大約十種類型擾動的穩健性評估基準，並在傳統和代理設置下進行評估。我們評估了多個最先進的大型語言模型 (LLMs)，包括 Grok-4.1、Gemini-3-Pro、Claude-Opus-4.6 和 GPT-5.2。我們的結果顯示，這些模型在多種擾動下通常保持強勁的性能；然而，對於表面層的噪音（例如，字符級的損壞）和在改變詞彙或句法形式的同時保留語義的語言變異，則觀察到明顯的性能下降。此外，我們觀察到表面層的噪音在傳統管道中導致更大的性能下降，而語言變異在代理設置中則帶來更大的挑戰。這些發現突顯了在實現穩健的 NL2SQL 系統方面仍然存在的挑戰，特別是在處理語言變異性時。
2603.17017.author: Lifu Tu et.al.
2603.17017.authors: Lifu Tu, Rongguang Wang, Tao Sheng, Sujjith Ravi, Dan Roth
